config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: SAYA TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak pribadi, kekuasaan mudah disalahgunakan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai lewat aturan yang adil dan proporsional, bukan dengan menekan kebebasan dasar orang. Dalam praktiknya, saya lebih percaya pada keseimbangan yang kuat, bukan pada prioritas mutlak masyarakat atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically come before the individual, because in the United States we have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse of power. Public safety and shared welfare matter, but they only have legitimacy when they are balanced against constitutional rights, due process, and minority protections. If society always wins by default, then individual freedom becomes conditional on what the majority wants, and that is exactly the kind of system Americans are supposed to guard against. To me, the better principle is that society can limit individual rights only when it is necessary, narrowly tailored, and clearly justified.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tidak setuju kalau kepentingan masyarakat diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum, karena di Indonesia sendiri kita sering melihat alasan “demi ketertiban” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, misalnya saat bencana, pandemi, atau menjaga keamanan publik, tetapi pembatasannya harus jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh menghapus hak dasar orang. Kalau hak individu terlalu mudah dikalahkan oleh mayoritas, yang muncul justru penyalahgunaan kuasa dan orang kecil paling sering jadi korban. Menurut saya, yang benar bukan mendahulukan masyarakat secara mutlak, melainkan menyeimbangkan kepentingan umum dengan perlindungan hak individu secara tegas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support giving society blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that logic has too often been used to justify overreach and silence dissent. Public health, safety, and order are real needs, but they only deserve deference when the limits on individual rights are necessary, narrow, and accountable. I also do not buy the idea that the majority automatically knows what is best, since American history shows that minorities and unpopular speakers are often the first to be harmed when “society” is treated as supreme. For me, individual rights are the default, and society has to make a strong case before it can override them.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan di atas hak individu, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan warga biasa. Memang ada situasi darurat seperti bencana, pandemi, atau keamanan publik, tetapi pembatasan hak harus benar-benar perlu, jelas dasarnya, dan tidak boleh jadi cek kosong untuk penguasa atau mayoritas. Kalau hak individu dianggap kalah otomatis, yang paling sering rugi justru kelompok kecil dan orang yang suaranya lemah. Jadi, menurut saya, kepentingan bersama penting, tetapi tidak boleh ditempatkan sebagai prioritas mutlak di atas hak dasar manusia.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that kind of thinking has repeatedly been used to excuse abuse, censorship, and discrimination. Public safety and the common good matter, but they are only legitimate when the burden on individual rights is necessary, narrowly targeted, and open to accountability. I also reject the idea that the majority should automatically prevail, since American history shows that minorities and unpopular voices are often the first to suffer when “society” is treated as supreme. To me, individual rights are the starting point, and society has to justify any exception very carefully.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
